QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $719.79 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Thursday's US session saw SPY gain 0.6% to 748.17, QQQ edge up 0.2% to 719.79, and the VIX ease to 17.26, though inflation fears persisted after the 3.8% April CPI print. Oil rallied 1.0% to $103.39 on fresh Hormuz ship seizures, while the Trump-Xi summit concluded without a trade deal, but both sides weighed managed tariff cuts on $30 billion of imports.
commodities_note: WTI climbed to $103.39 on escalating ship seizures and a tanker fuel leak, keeping the supply-disruption premium intact. Gold fell 0.9% to $4,582.40. The US10Y yield edged down to 4.46% as safe-haven demand competed with hotter inflation. Note: QQQ priced at $719.79, above its historical range — verify data source for possible unit mismatch or rollover.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain CAUTION with overbought RSI readings. DBS is the most extended at +6.7% above its 50-day, while UOB holds the lowest RSI at 65.2 but is still hard-blocked. NIM compression from high US10Y (4.46%) and MAS S$NEER appreciation persists with no relief until October.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — awaiting US10Y below 4.20% or MAS easing
singapore.key_development: US10Y held above 4.46% for the 57th day, with no rate relief in sight; MAS's April decision maintained S$NEER pace, pushing the next catalyst to October 2026.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX slipped 0.3% to 530.03 as the entire cluster remains CAUTION. Extreme extensions dominate: AMD +64%, INTC +70%, MU +62%, SK Hynix +58%, while NVDA added 3.5% to +23%. The Cisco AI networking surge (shares +15%) lifted AVGO +5.3% and LITE, but no ticker is in an entry zone.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — every name blocked by >+10% extension
semiconductors.key_development: Cisco's report of $5.3B AI infrastructure orders and raised fiscal-year guidance sparked a networking sub-rally within semis, validating AI infrastructure demand but exhausting near-term entry setups.

big_tech.summary: MSFT is the only BigTech name in HOLD, sitting 2.7% above its 50-day with R:R 1.5:1 — near an upgrade trigger. AMZN and GOOG remain hard-blocked CAUTION with extreme extensions above +13%, while PLTR stays CAUTION on a 65x P/E despite a slight bounce.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT near entry, the rest blocked by extension or valuation
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft's OpenAI concentration risk remains the structural overhang, but the non-OpenAI commercial RPO (+28%) provides a cleaner demand signal; a break above $413.05 on volume could move MSFT to WATCH.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier; Samsung qualification status with NVDA directly affects memory supply diversity and pricing for MU and SK Hynix.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung confirms HBM3E qualification in Q2 earnings (April 30 call), potentially breaking the duopoly and repricing SK Hynix/MU.

interconnected.name: Cadence Design Systems
interconnected.reason: US EDA software export restrictions to China read through to ASML's DUV/EUV revenue and INTC's foundry ambitions — any relaxation lifts the entire advanced-node supply chain.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if the Trump-Xi summit leads to a concrete EDA license relaxation, or after US Commerce Department guidance in July.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI data center networking upgrades benefit Arista alongside Cisco; NOK's optical thesis shares read-through on hyperscaler switch spend.
interconnected.entry_note: Worth adding if Cisco's AI order surge translates into enterprise switching demand, confirmed at next quarterly report (late July).

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA photonics investment (per NVIDIA IR, March 2, 2026), directly tied to LITE's optics ramp and AI datacenter interconnect demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for pullback to $X after Q3 earnings (May 6) if photonics revenue exceeds $600M quarterly, confirming NVDA supply-chain lock-in.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz blockade: fresh ship seizures and a drone-damaged tanker leaking fuel escalate the crisis, keeping WTI above $103 (per Reuters, May 14).

Trump-Xi summit concluded without a trade deal, but both sides discuss managed tariff cuts on $30B of imports; US clears H200 chip sales to 10 Chinese firms but no deliveries made (per Reuters, May 14).
Fed hawkish pivot: Boston Fed’s Collins says rate hikes may be needed to quell inflation; April CPI at 3.8% headline fuels tightening bets (per Yahoo Finance, May 13).
US-China tech flashpoints remain unresolved: semiconductor export controls, critical rare earth minerals, and EV market access cause uncertainty (per CNBC, May 14).
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil is pressuring logistics and inflation, keeping rates high and extending the Singapore bank NIM compression. Semis rally on AI demand despite geopolitical tension, but extreme extensions leave no safe entry points. Tech earnings in the next two weeks (NVDA, AVGO) are critical to sustaining the AI trade.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Two fresh ship seizures and a fuel leak escalated Hormuz disruption. Trump and Xi agreed to explore a Board of Trade for managed tariff cuts but signed no concrete deal. Fed’s Collins publicly stated rate hikes may be needed.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, no new entries in extended semis; monitor MSFT for an upgrade trigger and WRD for a starter position under tight risk controls.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — only 1 of 22 names actionable, 19 blocked by technical extension
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Nineteen of 22 names are hard-blocked on extension, with semis uniformly above +10% and three Singapore banks overbought. The lone ACCUMULATE (WRD) is a speculative ADR with delisting risk, keeping overall exposure minimal.
